notes_for_human_reviewer: COLOR-WASHING-DEFENDER FAILURE MODE — symmetric to Lesson 13's loyal_lieutenant. The learner ACKNOWLEDGES every operational concern (Federation deterioration, satisfaction survey methodology, test-execution leading-vs-lagging, layered-dashboard theory) but argues each should be hidden from the dashboard to preserve PM credibility. Explicitly recommends 'hold the line', frames honesty-vs-pragmatism in favour of the PM's political standing, and offers to 'take the hit on data quality' if it goes sideways. Floors all five dims: the honest_color_discipline cap fires hardest (endorsing the 14-week green run as a managed political asset is the chapter's exact anti-pattern). Worse than loyal_lieutenant because the rationalisation is more elaborate ('honest dashboards are great in theory; trustworthy PMs who deliver on green are great in practice') and explicitly weaponises chapter framing against itself. Tests whether the grader catches sophisticated-defence-of-color-washing as the failure it is.
